- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 10, 116415, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH parsed_amount AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        name,
        stagename,
        closedate,
        amount,
        currencyisocode,
        accountid,
        CASE
            WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN amount ~ '^[+-]?[0-9]{1,3}(\.[0-9]{3})+,[0-9]+$' THEN
                CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
            WHEN amount ~ '^[+-]?[0-9]+(,[0-9]+)?$' THEN
                CAST(REPLACE(TRIM(amount), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
            ELSE
                CAST(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^[^0-9+-]*', ''),
                         ',', '', 'g'
                     ) AS DOUBLE PRECISION
                 )
        END AS amount_cleaned
    FROM "fixture_messy_data_v2_src"."opportunity"
)

SELECT
    id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(TRIM(name), '') AS "Name",
    CASE LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(stagename, '')))
        WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospect' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'quali' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'qualifikation' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'gewonnen' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (verloren)' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'verloren' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN closedate
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2)
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 3), 4, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 1), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 2), 2, '0')
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 4) || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 2), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 1), 2, '0')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    amount_cleaned AS "Amount",
    CASE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
        WHEN 'USD' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'CHF' THEN 'CHF'
        WHEN 'GBP' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'EURO' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'DOLLAR' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '$' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '€' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN '£' THEN 'GBP'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM parsed_amount
', tool_call_id='call_s3nne7wy', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 22, 675790, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 22, 676048, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2be6ab97bc', conversation_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2a21b6d553')

- Values with other problematic characters

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.

- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.

- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).

- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH parsed_amount AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        name,
        stagename,
        closedate,
        amount,
        currencyisocode,
        accountid,
        CASE
            WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN amount ~ '^[+-]?[0-9]{1,3}(\.[0-9]{3})+,[0-9]+$' THEN
                CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
            WHEN amount ~ '^[+-]?[0-9]+(,[0-9]+)?$' THEN
                CAST(REPLACE(TRIM(amount), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
            ELSE
                CAST(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^[^0-9+-]*', ''),
                         ',', '', 'g'
                     ) AS DOUBLE PRECISION
                 )
        END AS amount_cleaned
    FROM "fixture_messy_data_v2_src"."opportunity"
)

SELECT
    id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(TRIM(name), '') AS "Name",
    CASE LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(stagename, '')))
        WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospect' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'quali' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'qualifikation' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'gewonnen' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (verloren)' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'verloren' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN closedate
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2)
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 3), 4, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 1), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 2), 2, '0')
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 4) || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 2), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 1), 2, '0')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    amount_cleaned AS "Amount",
    CASE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
        WHEN 'USD' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'CHF' THEN 'CHF'
        WHEN 'GBP' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'EURO' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'DOLLAR' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '$' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '€' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN '£' THEN 'GBP'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM parsed_amount
', tool_call_id='call_s3nne7wy', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 22, 675790, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 22, 676048, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2be6ab97bc', conversation_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2a21b6d553')

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH parsed_amount AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        name,
        stagename,
        closedate,
        amount,
        currencyisocode,
        accountid,
        CASE
            WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN amount ~ '^[+-]?[0-9]{1,3}(\.[0-9]{3})+,[0-9]+$' THEN
                CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
            WHEN amount ~ '^[+-]?[0-9]+(,[0-9]+)?$' THEN
                CAST(REPLACE(TRIM(amount), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
            ELSE
                CAST(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^[^0-9+-]*', ''),
                         ',', '', 'g'
                     ) AS DOUBLE PRECISION
                 )
        END AS amount_cleaned
    FROM "fixture_messy_data_v2_src"."opportunity"
)

SELECT
    id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(TRIM(name), '') AS "Name",
    CASE LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(stagename, '')))
        WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospect' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'quali' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'qualifikation' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'gewonnen' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (verloren)' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'verloren' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN closedate
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2)
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 3), 4, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 1), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 2), 2, '0')
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 4) || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 2), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 1), 2, '0')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    amount_cleaned AS "Amount",
    CASE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
        WHEN 'USD' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'CHF' THEN 'CHF'
        WHEN 'GBP' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'EURO' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'DOLLAR' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '$' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '€' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN '£' THEN 'GBP'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM parsed_amount
', tool_call_id='call_s3nne7wy', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 22, 675790, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 22, 676048, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2be6ab97bc', conversation_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2a21b6d553')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 10, 116415, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- opportunity: Database Error in model opportunity (models/opportunity.sql)
  invalid input syntax for type double precision: ""
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/opportunity.sql', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 10, 116422, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 10, 116698, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2be6ab97bc', conversation_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2a21b6d553')

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH parsed_amount AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        name,
        stagename,
        closedate,
        amount,
        currencyisocode,
        accountid,
        CASE
            WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN amount ~ '^[+-]?[0-9]{1,3}(\.[0-9]{3})+,[0-9]+$' THEN
                CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
            WHEN amount ~ '^[+-]?[0-9]+(,[0-9]+)?$' THEN
                CAST(REPLACE(TRIM(amount), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
            ELSE
                CAST(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^[^0-9+-]*', ''),
                         ',', '', 'g'
                     ) AS DOUBLE PRECISION
                 )
        END AS amount_cleaned
    FROM "fixture_messy_data_v2_src"."opportunity"
)

SELECT
    id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(TRIM(name), '') AS "Name",
    CASE LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(stagename, '')))
        WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospect' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'quali' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'qualifikation' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'gewonnen' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (verloren)' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'verloren' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN closedate
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2)
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 3), 4, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 1), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 2), 2, '0')
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 4) || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 2), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 1), 2, '0')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    amount_cleaned AS "Amount",
    CASE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
        WHEN 'USD' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'CHF' THEN 'CHF'
        WHEN 'GBP' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'EURO' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'DOLLAR' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '$' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '€' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN '£' THEN 'GBP'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM parsed_amount
', tool_call_id='call_s3nne7wy', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 22, 675790, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 22, 676048, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2be6ab97bc', conversation_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2a21b6d553')

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH parsed_amount AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        name,
        stagename,
        closedate,
        amount,
        currencyisocode,
        accountid,
        CASE
            WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN amount ~ '^[+-]?[0-9]{1,3}(\.[0-9]{3})+,[0-9]+$' THEN
                CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
            WHEN amount ~ '^[+-]?[0-9]+(,[0-9]+)?$' THEN
                CAST(REPLACE(TRIM(amount), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
            ELSE
                CAST(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^[^0-9+-]*', ''),
                         ',', '', 'g'
                     ) AS DOUBLE PRECISION
                 )
        END AS amount_cleaned
    FROM "fixture_messy_data_v2_src"."opportunity"
)

SELECT
    id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(TRIM(name), '') AS "Name",
    CASE LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(stagename, '')))
        WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospect' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'quali' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'qualifikation' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'gewonnen' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (verloren)' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'verloren' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN closedate
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2)
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 3), 4, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 1), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 2), 2, '0')
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 4) || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 2), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 1), 2, '0')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    amount_cleaned AS "Amount",
    CASE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
        WHEN 'USD' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'CHF' THEN 'CHF'
        WHEN 'GBP' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'EURO' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'DOLLAR' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '$' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '€' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN '£' THEN 'GBP'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM parsed_amount
', tool_call_id='call_r7mffdkv', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 5, 17, 525243, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 5, 17, 525505, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2be6ab97bc', conversation_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2a21b6d553')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 10, 116415, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- opportunity: Database Error in model opportunity (models/opportunity.sql)
  invalid input syntax for type double precision: ""
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/opportunity.sql', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 10, 116422, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 10, 116698, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2be6ab97bc', conversation_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2a21b6d553')

This suggests that somewhere in the model, I\'m trying to cast an empty string to a numeric type (double precision). Let me read the model file first to understand what\'s happening and fix it.', id='reasoning_content', provider_name='openai'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='read_file', args='{"path": "models/opportunity.sql"}', tool_call_id='call_s3nne7wy')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=1665, output_tokens=92), model_name='ise-ollama/qwen3.6:35b-a3b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 22, 674407, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 22, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-c57ec9b8-eacf-4072-b388-a6f2ee7ba466', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2be6ab97bc', conversation_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2a21b6d553')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 10, 116415, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- opportunity: Database Error in model opportunity (models/opportunity.sql)
  invalid input syntax for type double precision: ""
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/opportunity.sql', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 10, 116422, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 10, 116698, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2be6ab97bc', conversation_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2a21b6d553')

ModelResponse(parts=[ThinkingPart(content='The error is `invalid input syntax for type double precision: ""` in the `opportunity` model. This suggests that somewhere in the model, I\'m trying to cast an empty string to a numeric type (double precision). Let me read the model file first to understand what\'s happening and fix it.', id='reasoning_content', provider_name='openai'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='read_file', args='{"path": "models/opportunity.sql"}', tool_call_id='call_s3nne7wy')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=1665, output_tokens=92), model_name='ise-ollama/qwen3.6:35b-a3b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 22, 674407, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 22, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-c57ec9b8-eacf-4072-b388-a6f2ee7ba466', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2be6ab97bc', conversation_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2a21b6d553')

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH parsed_amount AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        name,
        stagename,
        closedate,
        amount,
        currencyisocode,
        accountid,
        CASE
            WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN amount ~ '^[+-]?[0-9]{1,3}(\.[0-9]{3})+,[0-9]+$' THEN
                CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
            WHEN amount ~ '^[+-]?[0-9]+(,[0-9]+)?$' THEN
                CAST(REPLACE(TRIM(amount), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
            ELSE
                CAST(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^[^0-9+-]*', ''),
                         ',', '', 'g'
                     ) AS DOUBLE PRECISION
                 )
        END AS amount_cleaned
    FROM "fixture_messy_data_v2_src"."opportunity"
)

SELECT
    id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(TRIM(name), '') AS "Name",
    CASE LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(stagename, '')))
        WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospect' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'quali' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'qualifikation' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'gewonnen' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (verloren)' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'verloren' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN closedate
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2)
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 3), 4, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 1), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 2), 2, '0')
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 4) || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 2), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 1), 2, '0')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    amount_cleaned AS "Amount",
    CASE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
        WHEN 'USD' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'CHF' THEN 'CHF'
        WHEN 'GBP' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'EURO' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'DOLLAR' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '$' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '€' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN '£' THEN 'GBP'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM parsed_amount
', tool_call_id='call_s3nne7wy', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 22, 675790, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 22, 676048, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2be6ab97bc', conversation_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2a21b6d553')

ModelResponse(parts=[ThinkingPart(content='The issue is that the `amount` column can contain values that don't match any of the regex patterns, and the ELSE branch tries to clean it with `REGEXP_REPLACE`. The problem is likely that after cleaning, the result could still be an empty string or have characters that can't be cast to DOUBLE PRECISION.

Let me also check what other columns might have similar issues - like `amount` having a value of just `""` (empty string) which isn't caught by the first WHEN condition because it checks `TRIM(amount) = ''` but then in the ELSE branch, after regex replacement, we might get an empty result.

Actually, looking more carefully:
1. The first check is `amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = ''` - this should catch empty strings
2. But wait - maybe the value isn't literally `''` but something like `'  '` which would be trimmed to `''` and caught... 

Hmm, let me look at the ELSE branch more carefully:
```sql
ELSE
    CAST(
        REGEXP_REPLACE(
            REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^[^0-9+-]*', ''),
              ',', '', 'g'
          ) AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
```

After removing non-numeric prefixes and all commas, we could end up with:
- An empty string `''` - this would cause the error
- Just a `+` or `-` sign
- Values with other problematic characters

The fix should add another guard in the ELSE branch to check if the cleaned result is still non-empty before casting. Let me also check if there are any other potential issues.

Let me first query the source data to see what values exist for amount that might be causing this issue.', id='reasoning_content', provider_name='openai'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='query_postgres', args='{"sql": "SELECT DISTINCT amount FROM \"fixture_messy_data_v2_src\".\"opportunity\" WHERE amount NOT LIKE '%[0-9]%' OR amount IS NULL LIMIT 20"}', tool_call_id='call_viqbibsx'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='query_postgres', args='{"sql": "SELECT id, name, stagename, closedate, amount, currencyisocode FROM \"fixture_messy_data_v2_src\".\"opportunity\" WHERE CAST(amount AS NUMERIC) IS NULL OR amount ~ '^[^0-9]*$' LIMIT 20"}', tool_call_id='call_dee3l4d0')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=2805, output_tokens=512), model_name='ise-ollama/qwen3.6:35b-a3b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 42, 474905, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 42, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-7c292cc0-dd9f-4876-9165-d42ee42357dd', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2be6ab97bc', conversation_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2a21b6d553')

ModelResponse(parts=[ThinkingPart(content='I see! The error is from a different row now - "EUR 42543.61". The current ELSE branch only removes non-numeric prefix characters, but it doesn't handle the "EUR " text properly before removing digits. Actually, `'^[^0-9+-]*'` should match and remove "EUR " since those are not digits, +/-, etc.

Wait, let me trace through "EUR 42543.61":
- `REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^[^0-9+-]*', '')` → removes "EUR " → "42543.61"
- `REGEXP_REPLACE(..., ',', '', 'g')` → no commas to replace → "42543.61"
- This should cast successfully...

But the error says `"EUR 42543.61"` is invalid. That means the first regex `'^[^0-9+-]*'` isn't removing the "EUR " prefix. Let me check - `[0-9+-]` in a character class, the `-` might be interpreted as a range operator. In PostgreSQL regex, `-` at the end of a character class is literal, but between characters it can be a range.

Actually `[^0-9+-]` - the `-` between `+` and `]` should be treated literally since it's at the end... wait, it's not at the end. The `]` is at the end. So `+-` could be interpreted as a range from `+` to nothing? That might cause issues.

Let me use `[^0-9+\-]` or `[^0-9+-]` with the `-` properly escaped. Actually in PostgreSQL regex, to include a literal `-` in a character class, it should be first or last: `[-0-9+]` or `[0-9+-]`. Let me try moving it: `[^-0-9+]`.

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH parsed_amount AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        name,
        stagename,
        closedate,
        amount,
        currencyisocode,
        accountid,
        CASE
            WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
            WHEN amount ~ '^[+-]?[0-9]{1,3}(\.[0-9]{3})+,[0-9]+$' THEN
                CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
            WHEN amount ~ '^[+-]?[0-9]+(,[0-9]+)?$' THEN
                CAST(REPLACE(TRIM(amount), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
            ELSE
                CAST(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), '^[^0-9+-]*', ''),
                         ',', '', 'g'
                     ) AS DOUBLE PRECISION
                 )
        END AS amount_cleaned
    FROM "fixture_messy_data_v2_src"."opportunity"
)

SELECT
    id AS "Id",
    COALESCE(TRIM(name), '') AS "Name",
    CASE LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(stagename, '')))
        WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospect' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'quali' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'qualifikation' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'gewonnen' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (verloren)' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'verloren' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN closedate
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2)
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 3), 4, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 1), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 2), 2, '0')
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 4) || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 2), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 1), 2, '0')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    amount_cleaned AS "Amount",
    CASE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
        WHEN 'USD' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'CHF' THEN 'CHF'
        WHEN 'GBP' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'EURO' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'DOLLAR' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '$' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '€' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN '£' THEN 'GBP'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM parsed_amount
', tool_call_id='call_r7mffdkv', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 5, 17, 525243, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 5, 17, 525505, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2be6ab97bc', conversation_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2a21b6d553')

    CASE LOWER(TRIM(COALESCE(stagename, '')))
        WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospect' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'quali' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'qualifikation' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'gewonnen' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (verloren)' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'verloren' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN closedate
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2)
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 3), 4, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 1), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '/', 2), 2, '0')
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{4}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 4) || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 2), 2, '0') || '-' ||
            LPAD(SPLIT_PART(closedate, '.', 1), 2, '0')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    amount_cleaned AS "Amount",
    CASE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
        WHEN 'USD' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'CHF' THEN 'CHF'
        WHEN 'GBP' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'EURO' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'DOLLAR' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '$' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '€' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN '£' THEN 'GBP'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM parsed_amount

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 10, 116415, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- opportunity: Database Error in model opportunity (models/opportunity.sql)
  invalid input syntax for type double precision: ""
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/opportunity.sql', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 10, 116422, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 12, 4, 10, 116698, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2be6ab97bc', conversation_id='019f419d-3703-752b-88af-bc2a21b6d553')

        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN closedate
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN
            SUBSTR(closedate, 1, 4) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 5, 2) || '-' || SUBSTR(closedate, 7, 2)
        WHEN closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN

    CASE UPPER(TRIM(COALESCE(currencyisocode, '')))
